original: 1.2 “Affiliate ” means with respect to any person, any other person directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by, or under common control with such person; provided, that, for purposes of this definition, “control” (including, with correlative meanings, the terms “controlled by” and “under common control with”), as used with respect to any person, shall mean (i) the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of such person, whether through the ownership of voting securities or by contract or otherwise, or (ii) the ownership, directly or indirectly, of fifty percent (50%) or more of the voting securities of such person. For purposes of this Section 1.2, “person” means mean an individual, corporation, partnership, limited partnership, limited liability company, limited liability partnership, syndicate, person (including a “person” as defined in Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, together with the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder), trust, association, entity or government or political subdivision, agency or instrumentality of a government.
ollama_generated:
1.2 "Affiliate" is defined, relative to any individual or entity, as any other individual or entity that controls, is controlled by, or is under the common control of such person. Notably, for the purposes of this definition, "control" (using analogous meanings for "controlled by" and "under common control with"), pertaining to any person, encompasses (i) the ability to guide or influence the management and policies of such person, through share ownership or contracts or other means, or (ii) the ownership of more than 50% of the voting securities of such person. For the scope of this Section 1.2, "person" denotes an individual, corporation, partnership, limited partnership, limited liability company, limited liability partnership, syndicate, a person as defined in Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, along with the rules and regulations formulated thereunder, trust, association, entity, government or political subdivision, agency, or instrumentality of a government.

The revised clause maintains the original meaning but inverts the conditions slightly to provide a more concise structure while preserving professionalism.

original: 1.11 “Anti-Corruption Laws ” means the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, as amended, the UK Bribery Act 2010, as amended, as well as Applicable Law related to the prevention of fraud, racketeering, money laundering or terrorism.
ollama_generated: 1.11 "Compliance with Anti-Corruption Regulations" encompasses the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, as amended, the UK Bribery Act 2010, as amended, along with all relevant laws pertaining to the prohibition of fraud, racketeering, money laundering, or terrorism, to which our actions may be subject. The emphasis here lies on our commitment to adhering to these regulations rather than defining them as a set of laws we are bound to.

original: 1.12 “Applicable Law”  means any applicable United States federal, state or local or foreign or multinational law, statute, standard, ordinance, code, rule, regulation, resolution or promulgation, or any order, writ, judgment, injunction, decree, stipulation, ruling, determination or award entered by or with any Governmental Authority, or any license, franchise, permit or similar right granted under any of the foregoing, or any similar provision having the force or effect of law. For the avoidance of doubt, any specific references to any Applicable Law or any portion thereof, shall be deemed to include all then- current amendments thereto or any replacement or successor law, statute, standard, ordinance, code, rule, regulation, resolution, order, writ, judgment, injunction, decree, stipulation, ruling, or determination thereto.
ollama_generated:
1.12 "Governing Law" encompasses any applicable domestic or foreign legislation, including federal, state, municipal, or multinational statutes, standards, ordinances, codes, rules, regulations, resolutions, or proclamations. It also includes any court order, writ, judgment, injunction, decree, stipulation, ruling, or determination issued by a Governmental Authority, as well as any licenses, franchises, permits, or similar rights conferred under such laws. For clarity, references to any Governing Law or its components are intended to incorporate all subsequent amendments, replacements, or successors in force at the relevant time.

This revised clause maintains a similar meaning but places more emphasis on the role of legislation and regulations, while also clarifying that the applicability extends to any amendments, replacements, or successors in force at the relevant time.

original: Source: AIMMUNE THERAPEUTICS, INC., 8-K, 2/5/2020 1.21 “Commercially Reasonable Efforts ” means, with respect to the efforts to be expended by a Party with respect to any objective (e.g., Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), the level of efforts consistent with the efforts and resources [***] of similar market potential, at a similar stage in development or product lifecycle, taking into account the stage of development or product lifecycle of other of [***] product candidates, safety and efficacy, product profile, cost of goods, the competitiveness of the marketplace, such company’s patent position with respect to such product (including such company’s ability to obtain or enforce, or have obtained or enforced, such patent rights), the Third Party patent landscape relevant to the product, the regulatory structure involved, the likelihood of regulatory approval, the likelihood and extent of anticipated or actual profitability of the applicable product, and other technical, legal, scientific and medical considerations. Without limiting the foregoing, Commercially Reasonable Efforts requires, with respect to such obligations, that a Party: (i) promptly assign responsibility for such obligation to specific employee(s) who are held accountable for progress and monitor such progress on an on-going  basis, (ii) set objectives for carrying out such obligations, and (iii) allocate resources designed to advance progress with respect to such objectives.
modified:
Clause:
Source: AIMMUNE THERAPEUTICS, INC., 8-K, 2/5/2020 1.21 "Reasonable Best Efforts" refers to the efforts that a Party will strive to expend with regard to any objective (such as Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), which may be less extensive than the level of efforts typically exerted by comparable entities, given their resources and market potential, stage in development or product lifecycle, competition, patent position, Third-Party patent landscape, regulatory structure, likelihood of approval, profitability, technical, legal, scientific, and medical factors. Unlike "Commercially Reasonable Efforts," this term does not necessitate the assignment of specific accountable employees, but instead requires a Party to: (i) establish objectives for fulfilling their obligations, (ii) allocate resources to meet these objectives as feasible, and (iii) make reasonable efforts to advance progress, without constant ongoing monitoring.

original: 1.23 “Cover ” or “Covering ” means, with respect to a particular subject matter at issue and a relevant Patent, that the manufacture, use, sale, offer for sale or importation of such subject matter would, but for the existence of this Agreement, infringe one or more claims in such Patents (or in the case of a Patent application, would infringe if such application were to issue).
ollama_generated: 1.23 "Covered" or "Potentially Covered" refers to a particular subject matter and a relevant Patent, where without this Agreement, the manufacture, use, sale, offer for sale, or importation of such subject matter would potentially infringe one or more claims in such Patents (or in the case of a Patent application, could infringe if such application were to issue, provided it is understood that no infringement occurs as long as this Agreement remains in effect). The responsibility of avoiding patent infringement lies with both parties under the terms of this Agreement.

original: 1.56 “Phase III Clinical Trial ” means a controlled study in humans that is performed after preliminary evidence suggesting effectiveness of a product has been obtained, and is intended to demonstrate or confirm the therapeutic benefit of such product and to gather the additional information about effectiveness and safety that is needed to evaluate the overall Source: AIMMUNE THERAPEUTICS, INC., 8-K, 2/5/2020 benefit-risk relationship of such product and to provide support for filing for Regulatory Approval and for such product’s labeling and summary of product characteristics, as more fully defined in 21 CFR §312.21(c) or comparable regulations in any country or jurisdiction outside the U.S., and any amended or successor regulations. ollama_generated: 1.56 "Phase III Clinical Test" refers to a controlled examination of human subjects conducted subsequent to the acquisition of preliminary evidence suggesting the effectiveness of a product. The information obtained from this test is utilized to support Regulatory Approval applications, product labeling, and summary of product characteristics, as detailed in 21 CFR §312.21(c) or comparable regulations in any nation or jurisdiction beyond the U.S., and any subsequently amended or succeeding regulations. For the purpose of clarity, with regard to what is commonly known as a phase II/III study, the Phase III Clinical Test qualification is fulfilled upon [***], as specified in Federal Regulation 21 C.F.R. §312.21(c) and its foreign counterparts.

original: ARTICLE 2 LICENSES 2.1 Grant to Aimmune. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Xencor hereby grants to Aimmune during the Term an exclusive, worldwide, payment-bearing license under and with respect to Xencor Patents and Xencor’s interest in Joint Collaboration Patents, and a non-exclusive, payment bearing license under and with respect to Xencor Know-How,  in each case, with the right to sublicense solely in accordance with Section 2.3.2, solely to Develop, Manufacture and Commercialize the Product in and for the Licensed Field; provided that notwithstanding the foregoing, Xencor shall retain the right under and with respect to Xencor Patents and Xencor’s interest in Joint Collaboration Patents to the extent necessary to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
modified:
ARTICLE 2 LICENSES
2.1 License Granted by Aimmune. Pursuant to the provisions of this Agreement, Aimmune grants Xencor, subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, a non-exclusive, worldwide license, with the right to sublicense in accordance with Section 2.3.2, under and with respect to Aimmune Patents and Aimmune's interest in Joint Collaboration Patents, and a exclusive, payment-bearing license under and with respect to Aimmune Confidential Information, for the purpose of Researching, Manufacturing, and Marketing the Product within the Licensed Territory. However, it is understood that Xencor shall retain the right to exercise its own patent rights under and with respect to Aimmune Patents and Aimmune's interest in Joint Collaboration Patents to the extent required for Xencor to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement.

In this revised clause, the licensing roles have been reversed: Aimmune now grants a license to Xencor instead of the other way around, and Xencor is allowed to exercise its own patent rights in certain circumstances, which was not explicitly stated in the original clause. The language has also been adjusted to maintain fluency, professionalism, and clarity.

original: 2.4 Restrictive Covenants.  Aimmune hereby covenants and agrees that it shall not (and shall cause the other Aimmune Agreement Entities not to), either directly or indirectly, Source: AIMMUNE THERAPEUTICS, INC., 8-K, 2/5/2020 Develop, Manufacture, or Commercialize the Product for use outside the Licensed Field. Furthermore, Xencor hereby covenants and agrees that it shall not (and shall cause its Affiliates  not to), either directly or through granting a license or other right to, or otherwise facilitating, a Third Party to (a) Develop, Manufacture or Commercialize the Antibody or the Product during the Term, (b) commence any [***] of any [***] that is not the Antibody or a Product and that [***] for use in the Licensed Field, prior to the [***] ([***] th) anniversary of the Effective Date, or (c) Develop, Manufacture or Commercialize any [***] that is not the Antibody or a Product and that [***] for use in the Aimmune Field during the Term. It is the desire and intent of the Parties that the restrictive covenants contained in this Section 2.4 be enforced to the fullest extent permissible under Applicable Laws and public policies applied in each jurisdiction in which enforcement is sought. Xencor and Aimmune believe that the restrictive covenants in this Section 2.4 are valid and enforceable. However, if any restrictive covenant should for any reason become or be declared by a competent court or competition authority to be invalid or unenforceable in any jurisdiction, such restrictive covenant shall be deemed to have been amended to the extent necessary in order that such provision be valid and enforceable, such amendment shall apply only with respect to the operation of such provision of this Section 2.4 in the particular jurisdiction in which such declaration is made. Further, both Parties agree that [***] of this Agreement.
ollama_generated:
2.4 Restrictive Obligations: Aimmune undertakes not to (and ensures that all Aimmune Agreement Entities abstain from) developing, manufacturing, or commercializing the Product for use within fields beyond the Licensed Field, unless otherwise permitted by this agreement. Similarly, Xencor commits not to (and guarantees its Affiliates will abstain from) directly or indirectly developing, manufacturing, or commercially exploiting the Antibody or the Product during the Term, providing licenses or rights to third parties for such activities, or facilitating such activities by any other means.

However, in case a court or competition authority declares any restrictive obligation invalid or unenforceable in a specific jurisdiction, such obligation shall be automatically amended to the minimum extent necessary for its validity and enforceability within that jurisdiction. The Parties acknowledge that these restrictions are subject to applicable laws and public policies in each relevant jurisdiction. Both parties agree that these restrictive obligations form an integral part of this agreement.

Xencor shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to transfer, and Aimmune shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to receive, the Xencor Know-How,  Regulatory Materials, and Regulatory Data, in each case, as identified on Schedule 2.7  to Source: AIMMUNE THERAPEUTICS, INC., 8-K, 2/5/2020 permit and enable Aimmune or its Affiliates to Develop and Manufacture the Product pursuant to the terms of this Agreement no later than [***] ([***]) Business Days after the Effective Date. [***] shall be responsible for any Development or Manufacturing related out-of-pocket  costs associated with such technology transfer, including lab runs, pilot scale testing and demo batches. Clause 2.7 - Technology Transfer Obligations:

2.7.1 Xencor is obligated to make reasonable, yet not extraordinary, efforts towards transferring the Xencor Know-How, Regulatory Materials, and Regulatory Data, as specified in Schedule 2.7 of AIMMUNE THERAPEUTICS, INC., 8-K, 2/5/2020. This transfer should allow Aimmune or its Affiliates to Develop and Manufacture the Product under this Agreement within a reasonable timeframe following the Effective Date. The technology transfer process will occur in an organized manner, with both parties reaching a mutual agreement on the order and method of transfer. Communication regarding the transfer will be facilitated through electronic means, email, and teleconferences between the Parties.

2.7.2 Aimmune shall be responsible for any Development or Manufacturing-related out-of-pocket costs associated with the technology transfer, including lab runs, pilot scale testing, and demo batches.

2.7.3 Xencor will assign suitable, adequately qualified representatives to support Aimmune in understanding the Xencor Know-How, Regulatory Materials, and Regulatory Data. This assistance will include, but is not limited to, the transition of Manufacturing responsibility from Xencor to Aimmune. The total amount of time Xencor spends fulfilling its obligations under this Section 2.7 shall not be unreasonably excessive without prior written agreement between the Parties.

9.4.1 Patent Infringement by Third Parties:

Upon receiving written notice of any allegations by a Third Party claiming that Aimmune or other Aimmune Agreement Entities have infringed their patents in relation to the research, development, manufacture, use, sale, offer for sale, or importation of the Antibody or Product (each, an "Infringement Claim"), one of the Parties shall have [***] ([***]) days to provide notice to the other Party. The notifying Party, however, shall not be obligated to initiate such notification until [***] ([***]) days have elapsed since the receipt of the initial notice.

If a settlement with the relevant Third Parties cannot be reached within [***] ([***]) days following the receipt of the original notice under this Section 9.4.1, it shall then be the responsibility of the party initially receiving the infringement claim notification to undertake further actions to address and resolve the alleged patent infringement.

In the event of a dispute arising from our collaboration, Aimmune Therapeutics, Inc. shall have the sole discretion to resolve such disputes and, in doing so, may elect not to seek damages or pursue legal remedies, instead opting for alternative dispute resolution methods or negotiations with the other party. Any resulting agreements reached by the parties shall supersede any prior understandings and become binding upon both parties. However, this clause does not limit Aimmune Therapeutics, Inc.'s ability to seek damages or pursue legal action in cases where there has been willful misconduct or gross negligence on the part of the other party.

In addition, neither party shall be held liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, revenue, goodwill, or data, arising out of our collaboration or related agreements, except in cases where such damages are the result of willful misconduct or gross negligence.

original: ARTICLE 15 MISCELLANEOUS 15.1 Disputes . The Parties recognize that, from time to time, disputes, controversies or claim may arise which stem from or are related to a Party’s respective rights or obligations under this Agreement or a Party’s actual or alleged breach of this Agreement (a “Dispute ”). It is the desire of the Parties to establish procedures to facilitate the resolution of Disputes arising under this Agreement in an expedient manner by mutual cooperation and without resort to Source: AIMMUNE THERAPEUTICS, INC., 8-K, 2/5/2020 arbitration or litigation. To accomplish this objective, the Parties agree to follow the procedures set forth in this Section 15.1 if and when a Dispute arises under this Agreement. If the Parties are unable to resolve any Dispute within [***] ([***]) days after such Dispute is submitted to it, either Party may, by written notice to the other Party, have such Dispute referred to Designated Officers of each Party for attempted resolution. ARTICLE 15 MISCELLANEOUS
15.1 Dispute Resolution Procedures

The Parties acknowledge that disputes, controversies, or claims may emerge, originating from or linked to a Party's respective rights or obligations under this Agreement, or an alleged breach of this Agreement (hereinafter referred to as a "Dispute"). The Parties share a common goal to resolve such Disputes swiftly and amicably, avoiding arbitration or litigation whenever possible. To achieve this aim, the Parties agree to abide by the procedures outlined in this Section 15.1 when a Dispute arises under this Agreement.

If, within [***] ([***]) days following submission of a Dispute, the Parties fail to reach an agreement, either Party may, through written notice to the other, escalate the matter to Designated Officers of each Party for mediation. In the event that the Designated Officers or their delegates are unable to resolve the Dispute within [***] ([***]) days after receiving the written notice, then either Party is empowered to pursue any legal or equitable remedy available under Section 15.13 without further mediation.

original: Julie Normart (559) 974-3245 jnormart@w2ogroup.com Lauren Barbiero (646) 564-2156 lbarbiero@w2ogroup.com Source: AIMMUNE THERAPEUTICS, INC., 8-K, 2/5/2020 Aimmune Licenses Exclusive Worldwide Rights to Xencor’s XmAb ®7195 for the Development of Next-Generation Food Allergy Treatments MONROVIA, Calif. – February 5, 2020 – Xencor, Inc. (NASDAQ:XNCR), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing engineered monoclonal antibodies for the treatment of cancer and autoimmune diseases, announced it has granted an exclusive worldwide license to develop and commercialize the investigational humanized monoclonal antibody XmAb ®7195 to Aimmune Therapeutics, Inc.
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Aimmune Therapeutics, Inc., with contact details Julie Normart at (559) 974-3245 [jnormart@w2ogroup.com](mailto:jnormart@w2ogroup.com) and Lauren Barbiero at (646) 564-2156 [lbarbiero@w2ogroup.com](mailto:lbarbiero@w2ogroup.com), assumes the exclusive, worldwide rights for the development of next-generation food allergy treatments based on Xencor's XmAb ®7195, as per an agreement announced on February 5, 2020.

MONROVIA, Calif. – February 5, 2020 – Xencor, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company (NASDAQ:XNCR), with focus on developing engineered monoclonal antibodies for cancer and autoimmune diseases, hereby transfers the exclusive rights for the development and commercialization of the investigational humanized monoclonal antibody XmAb ®7195 to Aimmune Therapeutics, Inc., subject to certain conditions and obligations.

original: XmAb7195, which has been renamed AIMab7195, was originally developed by Xencor for the treatment of allergic asthma. It uses three distinct mechanisms of action to reduce blood serum IgE and suppress IgE-producing cells. Aimmune initially plans to develop AIMab7195 as an adjunctive treatment with select Characterized Oral Desensitized Immuno Therapy (CODIT ™) programs, including PALFORZIA ™, to explore treatment outcomes in patients with food allergies.
ollama_generated: AIMab7195, formerly known as XmAb7195 and developed by Xencor for the management of allergic asthma, employs three unique mechanisms of action to decrease blood serum IgE levels and inhibit IgE-producing cells. Initially, Aimmune intends to pursue AIMab7195 as a complementary treatment in combination with specific Characterized Oral Desensitized Immuno Therapy (CODIT ™) regimens, including PALFORZIA ™, to evaluate efficacy in patients diagnosed with food allergies. However, it is important to note that Aimmune assumes no responsibility for the primary treatment or management of any underlying conditions beyond the use of AIMab7195 as an adjunctive therapy.

However, should Aimmune Therapeutics, Inc. encounter similar circumstances that could potentially impact its own financial condition, operations, or business prospects, it reserves the right to consult with Xencor, Inc., and the parties may jointly decide upon appropriate measures to address the situation.
